stories.title: Trump's 'Garden of Heroes' plans expand with pools, amphitheater, dining venues
stories.description: The latest blueprints could require a major redevelopment of West Potomac Park near the National Mall, the New York Times reports.

stories.title: Israeli strikes kill dozens in Lebanon despite ceasefire
stories.description: At least 44 people have been killed by Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon over the past two days, with both sides violating the April 17 truce.

stories.title: FEMA offers jobs back to workers cut under Noem
stories.description: The agency told a federal court it is reaching out to disaster workers whose contracts were not renewed in January, reversing cuts that sparked a lawsuit.

stories.title: Danco, DOJ ask Supreme Court to block abortion pill mail ban
stories.description: The emergency filings seek to pause a Fifth Circuit ruling that took effect Friday, requiring mifepristone to be dispensed only in person at clinics nationwide.

stories.title: Trump calls US Navy 'like pirates' enforcing Iran blockade
stories.description: CENTCOM says 45 ships have been turned back since the blockade began April 13, with Iran losing an estimated $5 billion in oil revenue.

stories.title: Spirit Airlines ceases all flights after $500M bailout collapses
stories.description: The 34-year-old ultra-low-cost carrier permanently shut down at 3 a.m. Saturday, leaving roughly 17,000 employees without jobs.

stories.title: NYPD arrests protesters who blockaded NYSE on May Day
stories.description: More than 100 demonstrators tried to shut down every entrance to the exchange as part of nationwide "Workers Over Billionaires" actions.

stories.title: Pew poll: Trump approval hits second-term low of 34%
stories.description: A new Pew Research Center survey finds sharp declines among Hispanic and young voters who helped power Trump's 2024 win.

stories.title: US warns EU allies of arms delivery delays as Iran war drains stockpiles
stories.description: The Financial Times reported Friday that warnings now extend beyond Baltic states to include the UK and Poland, as the conflict strains U.S. munitions reserves.

stories.title: Bard College president resigns after review of Epstein ties
stories.description: Leon Botstein, who led the college for 51 years, stepped down after investigators found he minimized his relationship with convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

stories.title: Trump endorses Rep. Barr in Kentucky Senate primary
stories.description: The endorsement reshapes the race to replace retiring Sen. Mitch McConnell less than three weeks before the May 19 GOP primary.

stories.title: US approves $8.6 billion in arms sales to Middle East allies
stories.description: The State Department on Friday cleared weapons deals with Israel, Qatar, Kuwait, and the UAE as the Iran ceasefire remains fragile.

stories.title: HHS visa delays leave hundreds of foreign doctors facing deportation
stories.description: Physicians contracted to work in underserved U.S. communities may have to leave the country if waiver applications aren't processed before a July 30 deadline.

stories.title: Trump says Medicare patients to get GLP-1 drugs by July
stories.description: The Bridge program will offer Wegovy and Zepbound to eligible seniors for a $50 monthly copay, operating outside the standard Part D benefit.

stories.title: Iran demands six Arab states pay reparations over war
stories.description: Tehran's UN envoy rejected Gulf states' self-defense claims, calling their role in the U.S.-Israeli campaign acts of aggression under international law.

stories.title: Ex-Rep. Rivera convicted in secret Venezuela lobbying case
stories.description: A Miami federal jury found Rivera and associate Esther Nuhfer guilty on all counts, including failing to register as foreign agents for the Maduro government.

stories.title: Pentagon to withdraw 5,000 troops from Germany
stories.description: The drawdown, set to take place over six to 12 months, escalates Trump's standoff with Chancellor Merz over the war in Iran.

stories.title: Kemp rules out redrawing Georgia maps before midterms
stories.description: The governor said voting is already underway and he won't cancel the May 19 primary, breaking from GOP counterparts in other states.

stories.title: Exxon and Chevron refuse to boost output despite White House pressure
stories.description: Both oil supermajors reported sharp profit declines but beat adjusted estimates, doubling down on cash flow over production growth amid the Iran war.

stories.title: Trump signs order expanding Cuba sanctions
stories.description: The executive order targets individuals and entities supporting Cuba's security apparatus amid an escalating maximum pressure campaign.

stories.title: Iran accelerates weapons recovery as ceasefire talks stall, NBC News reports
stories.description: U.S. officials say Tehran is exploiting the truce to dig out hidden missiles and drones as the prospect of renewed conflict grows.

stories.title: Pentagon strikes AI deals with Nvidia, Microsoft, and four other tech firms for classified work
stories.description: The Defense Department now has six major AI partnerships for classified military networks, following its bitter split with Anthropic earlier this year.

stories.title: US to shut Gaza ceasefire center as Trump plan stalls
stories.description: The CMCC in southern Israel will be folded into a smaller international force, cutting the U.S. military presence from around 190 troops to 40.

stories.title: Louisiana suspends House primaries after Supreme Court strikes down map
stories.description: Gov. Landry issued an executive order halting the state's congressional races after the court ruled its district map unconstitutional.

stories.title: Israel rushed Iron Beam laser system to UAE to counter Iran attacks
stories.description: The previously undisclosed deployment marks the first combat export of Israel's laser defense technology, which costs as little as $2 per shot.

stories.title: Trump says he's 'not satisfied' with Iran's latest proposal
stories.description: The president warned he could "blast the hell out of" Iran if diplomacy fails, as Tehran submitted a new offer through Pakistani mediators.

stories.title: Senate rejects House FISA bill, passes 45-day extension
stories.description: Lawmakers stripped a central bank digital currency ban that House Republicans attached, calling it a "poison pill" blocking surveillance renewal.

stories.title: UAE bans citizens from traveling to Iran, Lebanon, and Iraq
stories.description: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs urged Emiratis already in those countries to return immediately, citing regional security concerns amid the ongoing US-Iran conflict.

stories.title: Trump to raise tariffs on EU cars and trucks to 25%
stories.description: The president accused the EU of not complying with the Turnberry trade deal, which set a 15% ceiling on most goods including automobiles.

stories.title: China launches combat patrols near Scarborough Shoal amid US-Philippines drills
stories.description: Beijing's military and coast guard conducted naval and air operations in the disputed South China Sea as the largest-ever Balikatan exercises continue nearby.

stories.title: Senate unanimously bans members, staff from prediction markets
stories.description: The rule change takes effect immediately amid insider trading concerns on platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket.

stories.title: Tennessee rushes to redraw maps after SCOTUS voting rights ruling
stories.description: The president said Gov. Bill Lee agreed to work on eliminating the state's last Democratic House seat after the court weakened the Voting Rights Act.

stories.title: NYT seeks to unseal Epstein's alleged suicide note
stories.description: The New York Times filed a court request Thursday to release a note allegedly written by Epstein that has been sealed for nearly seven years.

stories.title: Camp Mystic withdraws license bid to reopen after fatal flood
stories.description: The all-girls camp in Hunt, Texas, abandoned plans for summer 2026 after weeks of pressure from lawmakers, the lieutenant governor and victims' families.

stories.title: Trump says he 'doesn't care' if Powell stays on Fed board
stories.description: A day after blasting the Fed chair on social media, the president struck a notably milder tone about Powell's decision to remain as a governor.

stories.title: U.S. economy grew 2% in Q1 as jobless claims hit 57-year low
stories.description: Thursday's data painted a mixed picture: GDP rebounded but missed forecasts, inflation accelerated sharply, and unemployment filings plunged to levels not seen since 1969.

stories.title: Maine Gov. Mills drops Senate bid, clearing path for Platner
stories.description: The two-term governor cited insufficient resources to continue against the insurgent oyster farmer, who led by double digits in polling.

stories.title: Trump says US has 'already won' Iran war, vows to hold blockade until nuclear deal
stories.description: Oil hit wartime highs above $120 a barrel as the president rejected Iran's latest peace proposal and insisted on permanent nuclear guarantees.

stories.title: US jobless claims drop to 189,000, near lowest since 1969
stories.description: The sharp weekly decline defies expectations of rising layoffs amid tariffs and geopolitical tensions, with filings falling well below forecasts.

stories.title: CENTCOM seeks first-ever Dark Eagle hypersonic missile use against Iran
stories.description: The request would mark the debut combat deployment of the $41 million-per-missile weapon, which still lacks full operational testing approval.

stories.title: DOJ pursuing third indictment of Comey over classified leaks
stories.description: Federal prosecutors have revived a probe into the former FBI director's dissemination of documents, days after he was charged over a seashell social media post.

stories.title: King Charles honors 9/11 victims in first royal NYC visit in 16 years
stories.description: The King and Queen laid a wreath at the National September 11 Memorial on Wednesday, meeting families and first responders ahead of the attacks' 25th anniversary.

stories.title: Pentagon releases $400M in Ukraine aid after McConnell pressure campaign
stories.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth confirmed the funds were released Wednesday, days after McConnell accused the department of letting the money "collect dust."

stories.title: FEMA reinstates whistleblowers sidelined under Noem
stories.description: Fifteen employees who signed the "Katrina Declaration" return to work as the agency prepares for hurricane season and the FIFA World Cup.

stories.title: London stabbing of 2 Jewish men declared terrorist attack
stories.description: Police arrested a 45-year-old suspect after two men were knifed in Golders Green, prompting PM Starmer to convene a COBRA meeting.

stories.title: USS Gerald R. Ford to leave Middle East after 10-month deployment
stories.description: The carrier's departure will reduce U.S. naval firepower in the region from three carrier strike groups to two amid the ongoing Iran conflict.

stories.title: Trump says WHCA dinner agent was shot by suspect, not friendly fire
stories.description: Ballistic analysis traced the round that struck the Secret Service agent's vest to Cole Allen's shotgun, ending days of speculation.

stories.title: Fed holds rates steady with most dissent since 1992
stories.description: The 8-4 vote split came at what is likely Chair Powell's final meeting, as oil-driven inflation clouds the rate outlook.

stories.title: Bondi agrees to May 29 deposition in House Epstein probe
stories.description: Democrats introduced a contempt resolution hours before the committee confirmed the former attorney general's new testimony date.

stories.title: Trump taps Nicole Saphier as surgeon general after pulling Casey Means
stories.description: Saphier, a radiologist at Memorial Sloan Kettering and former Fox News contributor, is Trump's third pick for the role in his second term.

stories.title: Hegseth tells Congress Iran's nuclear sites were 'obliterated'
stories.description: The defense secretary faced sharp pushback at a House hearing over why the U.S. remains at war if Iran's nuclear facilities were already destroyed.

stories.title: France unveils roadmap to end all fossil fuel use by 2050
stories.description: The plan, presented at a global summit in Colombia, sets deadlines to phase out coal by 2030, oil by 2045 and gas by 2050.

stories.title: Iran's Khamenei vows to defend nuclear capabilities after Trump-Putin call
stories.description: Iran's supreme leader called nuclear and missile programs "national assets," rejecting U.S. pressure as Trump pushes for a deal on Tehran's enriched uranium.

stories.title: Hegseth calls war critics 'biggest adversary' in fiery congressional testimony
stories.description: The defense secretary faced lawmakers for the first time since the Iran war began, as Pentagon revealed the conflict has cost $25 billion.

stories.title: Adelaide researchers map path to turning plastic into hydrogen fuel
stories.description: A new roadmap details how sunlight-powered photoreforming could convert waste plastics into clean hydrogen and industrial chemicals.

stories.title: Israel sets two-week deadline for Lebanon deal, warns of escalation
stories.description: Trump urged Netanyahu to conduct "surgical" strikes as Israel threatens to resume intensive operations if no permanent agreement is reached by mid-May.

stories.title: EU finds Meta failed to block under-13s from Instagram, Facebook
stories.description: The European Commission issued preliminary findings that Meta violated the Digital Services Act by not preventing children under 13 from accessing its platforms.

stories.title: Artemis II capsule returns to Kennedy as Artemis III rocket assembly begins
stories.description: NASA's Orion crew module arrived back at its launch site Tuesday while the next moon rocket's core stage entered the Vehicle Assembly Building.

stories.title: Appeals court rejects Trump bid to revisit E. Jean Carroll verdict
stories.description: The divided 2nd Circuit ruling leaves the Supreme Court as Trump's last option to challenge two jury verdicts totaling more than $88 million.

stories.title: King Charles urges unity in rare address to Congress
stories.description: The British monarch became only the second to speak before U.S. lawmakers, calling the alliance "one of the greatest partnerships in human history."

stories.title: Pentagon asks Congress to formally rename DOD the Department of War
stories.description: The legislative proposal would require 7,600 changes to existing law and has already cost an estimated $52 million, according to the Pentagon.

stories.title: DOJ unveils sweeping rollback of gun regulations
stories.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche called the 34 proposed and final rule changes the most comprehensive reform package in ATF history.

stories.title: Soldier pleads not guilty to betting on Maduro raid with classified intel
stories.description: Master Sgt. Gannon Ken Van Dyke faces five federal charges in what prosecutors call the first insider trading case involving a prediction market.

stories.title: GAO agrees to review DOJ's handling of Epstein files
stories.description: The independent watchdog's probe joins a DOJ inspector general audit and a new federal lawsuit over alleged noncompliance with the transparency law.

stories.title: Trump calls UAE's exit from OPEC 'great' for fuel prices
stories.description: The president praised the move as the White House met with oil executives to discuss sustaining the Iran blockade while shielding consumers.

stories.title: Over 23,000 Iranian businesses destroyed as war's economic toll mounts
stories.description: Two million jobs have already been lost, with half of Iran's workforce at risk as airstrikes devastate industry and inflation hits 72%.

stories.title: FBI raids 22 Minneapolis childcare centers in fraud probe
stories.description: No arrests were made, but agents executed search warrants at facilities tied to alleged COVID-era social services fraud totaling billions.

stories.title: Trump and Putin discuss Iran, Ukraine in 90-minute call
stories.description: The 90-minute conversation also covered the Iran conflict and the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting, the Kremlin said.

stories.title: SpaceX ties Musk's pay to Mars colony, $7.5T valuation
stories.description: A Reuters analysis reveals the compensation plan, disclosed in SpaceX's draft IPO prospectus, would award Musk 200 million super-voting shares.

stories.title: SPLC files motions challenging DOJ fraud case, seeks grand jury transcripts
stories.description: The civil rights group says its informant program was known to law enforcement and accuses acting AG Blanche of making false public statements.

stories.title: Warsh clears Senate committee, heads to full vote
stories.description: The Senate Banking Committee voted 13-11 along party lines to advance Trump's Fed chair nominee, putting him on track to succeed Powell before May 15.

stories.title: Google signs classified AI deal with Pentagon despite employee protests
stories.description: The agreement, reported Tuesday by The Information, came one day after more than 600 workers urged CEO Sundar Pichai to reject the contract.

stories.title: Iran war energy shock pushes millions back to charcoal and firewood
stories.description: Soaring fuel costs from the Strait of Hormuz closure are forcing families across Africa and South Asia to abandon cleaner cooking fuels, accelerating deforestation.

stories.title: Trump meets with oil execs as Iran blockade stalemate drags on
stories.description: The White House session with Chevron's CEO and other energy leaders came as Trump directed aides to prepare for a prolonged Strait of Hormuz naval blockade.

stories.title: Iraq taps political newcomer al-Zaidi as prime minister-designate
stories.description: President Amedi designated 40-year-old businessman Ali al-Zaidi to form a government after U.S. pressure blocked a pro-Iran candidate.

stories.title: OpenAI misses revenue and user targets ahead of IPO
stories.description: The shortfalls intensify doubts about the AI company's ability to sustain massive infrastructure spending as it prepares for a planned Q4 listing.

stories.title: Iran tensions shadow White House state dinner for British royals
stories.description: During Tuesday's state dinner, Trump said King Charles "agrees" Iran must never have a nuclear weapon, prompting a Palace response over the king's political neutrality.

stories.title: Airbnb.org offers free housing to Oklahomans displaced by tornadoes
stories.description: The initiative, announced Saturday in partnership with Heartline 211, follows a week of severe storms that damaged dozens of homes across the state.

stories.title: Appeals court lets Pentagon require escorts for journalists
stories.description: A divided D.C. Circuit panel stayed a lower court order, allowing the Defense Department to mandate journalist escorts while it appeals.

stories.title: State Dept. to issue passports with Trump's portrait for America's 250th birthday
stories.description: The limited-edition design makes Trump the first sitting president featured in a U.S. passport, available by request in Washington.

stories.title: First LNG tanker crosses Hormuz since Iran war began
stories.description: Ship-tracking data shows the vessel near India after weeks without a signal, but analysts caution one crossing doesn't signal a broader reopening.

stories.title: Judge pauses order requiring UPenn to share Jewish staff data with EEOC
stories.description: Penn will not have to turn over names of employees in Jewish organizations while it appeals to the Third Circuit.

stories.title: Supreme Court to hear challenge to Trump's TPS termination for Haitians, Syrians
stories.description: Oral arguments Wednesday in two consolidated cases could shape the future of temporary legal protections for more than 1 million immigrants from 17 countries.

stories.title: OpenAI, Microsoft end exclusive IP deal in revised pact
stories.description: The amended agreement lets OpenAI serve customers on any cloud platform and caps the revenue share it must pay Microsoft through 2030.

stories.title: Iran's Araghchi meets Putin as US peace talks stall
stories.description: Iran's foreign minister blamed Washington's "excessive demands" for the collapse of negotiations after arriving in St. Petersburg for talks with Putin.

stories.title: Trump signs law lifting mining ban near Boundary Waters
stories.description: The legislation reverses Biden-era protections on 225,000 acres of Minnesota's Superior National Forest, benefiting Chilean-owned Twin Metals.

stories.title: China blocks Meta's $2B acquisition of AI startup Manus
stories.description: The NDRC ordered the deal's cancellation Monday, citing laws on technology exports, as Beijing cracks down on "Singapore washing" strategies.

stories.title: China quietly expands economic pressure toolkit during trade truce
stories.description: Beijing has enacted new laws, tightened export controls, and banned foreign tech from key sectors even as it prepares for a mid-May summit with Washington.

stories.title: WHCD shooter charged with attempting to assassinate Trump
stories.description: Cole Tomas Allen was arraigned Monday on three federal counts, including attempted assassination, after Saturday's attack at the Washington Hilton.

stories.title: Hardliner set to replace Ghalibaf in Iran nuclear talks
stories.description: A leaked secret letter to Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei exposed a rift over whether Tehran should negotiate its nuclear program with the U.S.

stories.title: NYT traces U.S. Mint gold to Colombian drug cartel
stories.description: An investigation found the Mint buys gold from cartel-controlled mines and foreign pawn shops, violating a 1985 law requiring domestic sourcing.

stories.title: Reuters uncovers Trump push for federal election control in 8 states
stories.description: The investigation found the administration using FBI raids, DOJ lawsuits, and demands for voter data to assert control ahead of November's midterms.

stories.title: Global military spending hit record $2.89 trillion in 2025, SIPRI reports
stories.description: Europe's 14% surge and Asia's fastest growth since 2009 drove the 11th straight annual increase, even as U.S. spending fell.

stories.title: Zelenskyy says EU accession talks could open in June
stories.description: Ukraine and Moldova pressed the EU to begin formal membership negotiations, with Zelenskyy citing late May or June as the expected timeline.

stories.title: Trump calls CBS anchor 'a disgrace' over WHCD shooter manifesto question
stories.description: The president erupted at Norah O'Donnell during a Sunday "60 Minutes" interview after she read passages from the gunman's anti-Trump writings.

stories.title: Iran refuses talks unless US lifts naval blockade
stories.description: Tehran demands removal of the port blockade before resuming negotiations, as the U.S. Navy intercepts another sanctioned vessel.

stories.title: Zelenskyy says EU loan triggered Russia's 20-hour assault on Dnipro
stories.description: Speaking alongside Moldova's president in Kyiv, Zelenskyy called the deadly barrage a direct reaction to the EU unblocking €90 billion in support.

stories.title: California billionaire tax secures enough signatures for November ballot
stories.description: Backers say more than 1.5 million people signed the petition, far exceeding the roughly 875,000 valid signatures required.

stories.title: Trump tells Iran to call if it wants peace talks
stories.description: The president's remarks on Sunday came a day after he scrapped a planned trip by envoys Witkoff and Kushner to Pakistan for negotiations.

stories.title: Thousands gather on National Mall to honor Virginia Giuffre
stories.description: The "Butterfly Vigil" marks one year since the prominent Epstein accuser died by suicide at age 41 in Australia.

stories.title: Macron calls EU defense clause 'stronger' than NATO's
stories.description: The French president used an Athens visit to push European self-reliance while insisting the EU's defense buildup is not meant to replace NATO.

stories.title: Suspect in custody as Trump plans news conference after incident
stories.description: A suspect was apprehended and no injuries were reported after gunfire erupted at the Washington Hilton during the annual event Saturday night.

stories.title: Iran threatens 'decisive' military response to US Hormuz blockade
stories.description: Tehran's top military command said forces are at their "highest level of readiness" as three US carrier strike groups converge on the region.

stories.title: Chernobyl anniversary marked as IAEA warns damaged shield needs repair
stories.description: Ukraine commemorates the 1986 disaster on Sunday with the facility's protective shield still damaged from a 2025 Russian drone strike.

stories.title: White House official says Patel firing 'only a matter of time'
stories.description: A top White House official told Politico that FBI Director Kash Patel is likely the next Cabinet member to be removed amid mounting scandals.

stories.title: UK and Finland condemn Israeli killing of journalist in Lebanon as 'intolerable'
stories.description: The joint statement from the Media Freedom Coalition co-chairs follows the death of Al-Akhbar reporter Amal Khalil in an April 22 airstrike.

stories.title: Space Force awards $3.2B to 12 firms for Golden Dome interceptors
stories.description: The contracts task defense giants and startups with building space-based missile interceptors, a technically unproven pillar of the $185 billion shield.

stories.title: Trump fires all members of National Science Board
stories.description: The 25-member body governs the NSF and advises Congress on science policy. It was created by a 1950 federal law.

stories.title: US orders global diplomatic push over alleged Chinese AI theft
stories.description: The State Department cable names DeepSeek, Moonshot AI and MiniMax, and comes hours after DeepSeek released its V4 model.

stories.title: Palestinians vote in first Gaza election in 20 years
stories.description: Polls opened Saturday in Deir al-Balah and the West Bank for municipal elections, with Hamas effectively barred under a decree by President Abbas.

stories.title: Trump's ballroom builder got secret $17.4M no-bid deal
stories.description: Clark Construction, already tapped for Trump's White House ballroom, received a no-bid contract for Lafayette Park fountains at five times the prior cost estimate.

stories.title: Warren, Durbin warn DOJ could relaunch Powell probe at will
stories.description: Democratic senators say Pirro's decision to close the Fed investigation is only a "temporary pause," leaving the door open for a politically motivated revival.

stories.title: Hegseth brushes off Pope Leo's criticism of Iran war
stories.description: The defense secretary told reporters the military follows "the orders of the president," as the U.S.-Vatican rift over the conflict deepens.

stories.title: Judge questions jurisdiction over Trump's $10B IRS suit
stories.description: Judge Kathleen Williams ordered briefings on whether the case can proceed when the president controls both sides of the litigation.

stories.title: Nvidia surges toward record high as chip rally lifts sector
stories.description: Intel's earnings beat sparked a broad semiconductor rally, pushing Nvidia shares near their all-time closing high set in October 2025.

stories.title: Atlantic reporter says new sources emerged after Patel's $250M lawsuit
stories.description: FBI Director Kash Patel's defamation suit appears to have backfired as journalist Sarah Fitzpatrick says she's been "inundated" with corroborating sources.

stories.title: Trump administration moves to again revoke parole for CBP One migrants
stories.description: Court filings show new termination notices are being issued weeks after a judge ruled the first mass revocation was unlawful.

stories.title: Iran claims seized cargo ship was carrying dialysis supplies, demands release
stories.description: Tehran's UN mission and the Iranian Red Crescent say the Touska was carrying critical kidney dialysis materials when the US Navy seized it April 19.

stories.title: Netanyahu reveals secret prostate cancer diagnosis and treatment
stories.description: The Israeli prime minister said Friday he quietly underwent radiation therapy months ago and is now cancer-free and in "excellent physical condition."

stories.title: New York sues to reclaim $73M in highway funds cut by Trump administration
stories.description: Attorney General Letitia James and Gov. Hochul filed suit Friday after the DOT withheld funding over a dispute about commercial driver's licenses.

stories.title: Macron rejects US approach to Iran at EU summit
stories.description: The French president called for diplomacy over military pressure, warning that the Middle East conflict is destabilizing the global economy.

stories.title: California battery storage boom faces growing revolt from fire-wary communities
stories.description: Residents are blocking projects, filing lawsuits, and pushing moratoriums as developers seek sites near neighborhoods and wildfire zones.

stories.title: D.C. Circuit strikes down Trump's asylum ban at border
stories.description: The 2-1 ruling sets up a likely Supreme Court clash over whether the president can unilaterally bar migrants from seeking asylum.

stories.title: House panel advances bill cutting US UN funding by 78%
stories.description: The FY2027 spending bill would slash contributions to international organizations and zero out US dues for the UN regular budget.

stories.title: FDA clears first gene therapy for inherited hearing loss
stories.description: Regeneron says it will provide Otarmeni, which restored hearing in children born deaf, for free to eligible U.S. patients.

stories.title: DOJ expands federal execution methods to include firing squads
stories.description: Acting AG Todd Blanche directed the Bureau of Prisons to add firing squads and reinstate the lethal injection protocol from Trump's first term.

stories.title: Iran war strains US ability to arm and defend Taiwan
stories.description: A naval blockade, depleted munitions, and a stalled $14 billion arms deal are stretching Pentagon resources as China watches closely.

stories.title: DOJ targets 384 citizens for denaturalization in 'first wave'
stories.description: Prosecutors in 39 regional offices will pursue citizenship revocation cases, a sharp escalation from the historical average of 11 cases per year.

stories.title: 77% of voters blame Trump for gas price surge, poll finds
stories.description: A Reuters/Ipsos poll released Friday shows broad voter frustration over fuel costs that have risen more than 30% since the Iran war began.

stories.title: Meta to cut 8,000 jobs starting May 20, more layoffs planned for 2026
stories.description: The first wave targets about 10% of Meta's global workforce as the company pivots toward AI and away from divisions like Reality Labs.

stories.title: Wall Street slips from record highs as oil tops $103
stories.description: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq pulled back Thursday as Strait of Hormuz disruptions pushed Brent crude higher and U.S.-Iran peace talks stalled.

stories.title: Offshore wind farms expand off Rhode Island despite Trump administration's efforts to block them
stories.description: Three wind projects now operate in waters off Rhode Island as courts continue to reject federal attempts to halt the industry.

stories.title: JetBlue sued over alleged use of personal data to set fares
stories.description: A class action filed in Brooklyn federal court claims the airline uses tracking technology and customer data to inflate ticket prices.

stories.title: Microsoft offers first voluntary buyout in its 51-year history
stories.description: About 7% of U.S. employees are eligible for the retirement packages as the company continues reshaping its workforce around AI.

stories.title: Trump threatens UK with tariffs over digital services tax
stories.description: The warning came as new data showed the UK collected £944 million from the levy in 2025-26, a 17% increase from the prior year.

stories.title: France probes weather sensor tampering tied to Polymarket bets
stories.description: Météo-France filed a criminal complaint after suspicious temperature spikes at a Paris airport sensor aligned with $34,000 in prediction market wins.

stories.title: California's wildfire deforestation rivals global hotspots
stories.description: California lost conifer forest at up to three times the global average rate from 2001 to 2023, with reforestation efforts barely keeping pace, according to researchers.

stories.title: Trump weighs doubling refugee cap for white South Africans
stories.description: The administration is discussing adding 10,000 spots to the current 7,500 ceiling, nearly all earmarked for Afrikaners, according to Reuters.

stories.title: EU formally approves €90B Ukraine loan, new Russia sanctions
stories.description: Leaders gathering in Cyprus for a two-day summit cleared the long-stalled aid package and a 20th round of sanctions targeting Moscow's energy and financial sectors.

stories.title: Oklo and NVIDIA team up with Los Alamos on nuclear fuel for AI factories
stories.description: The partnership combines Oklo's reactor technology, NVIDIA's AI infrastructure, and the lab's materials science expertise to support the DOE's Genesis Mission.

stories.title: DOJ lost 11,200 employees despite tough-on-crime vows
stories.description: Internal records obtained by Reuters show the Justice Department's workforce shrank across nearly every division, including the FBI, DEA and ATF.

stories.title: Interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS traced to cold, ancient corner of the Milky Way
stories.description: Scientists say the third confirmed interstellar visitor likely formed up to 11 billion years ago in a frigid, isolated region before its host star's planetary system took shape.

stories.title: Senate adopts GOP budget plan to fund ICE, Border Patrol
stories.description: The 50-48 vote early Thursday advances a partisan $70 billion plan to reopen DHS, bypassing Democrats through budget reconciliation.

stories.title: China warns of 'critical juncture' as Iran ceasefire talks falter
stories.description: Beijing urged all parties to prevent a return to war after scheduled U.S.-Iran peace talks in Pakistan stalled and Trump extended the ceasefire while maintaining a naval blockade.

stories.title: SpaceX reveals in-house GPU plans in pre-IPO filing
stories.description: The company flagged chip supply risks and "substantial capital expenditures" for AI hardware as it prepares for a potential record-setting public offering.

stories.title: Tesla raises capex plan to $25 billion for AI push
stories.description: The EV maker now expects negative free cash flow for the rest of 2026 as it triples spending on robots, autonomy and AI infrastructure.

stories.title: DOJ reclassifies medical marijuana to Schedule III
stories.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche signed the order Thursday, delivering on Trump's December executive order and marking the biggest federal marijuana policy shift since 1970.

stories.title: Nuclear leaders convene in Busan, framing reactors as AI-era infrastructure
stories.description: Some 19,000 participants from 19 countries gathered in South Korea this week to discuss nuclear power's role in meeting surging AI electricity demand.

stories.title: IDF removes 40 Israeli settlers who crossed into Syria
stories.description: The far-right Pioneers of Bashan group barricaded themselves on a rooftop in a Syrian village, demanding approval for Jewish settlements.

stories.title: Trump says China helped push Iran toward ceasefire talks
stories.description: Beijing used its leverage as Iran's top oil buyer to urge Tehran back to the negotiating table, diplomats told the Associated Press.

top.title: Trump's 'Garden of Heroes' plans expand with pools, amphitheater, dining venues
top.description: The latest blueprints could require a major redevelopment of West Potomac Park near the National Mall, the New York Times reports.

top.title: Israeli strikes kill dozens in Lebanon despite ceasefire
top.description: At least 44 people have been killed by Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon over the past two days, with both sides violating the April 17 truce.

top.title: FEMA offers jobs back to workers cut under Noem
top.description: The agency told a federal court it is reaching out to disaster workers whose contracts were not renewed in January, reversing cuts that sparked a lawsuit.

top.title: Danco, DOJ ask Supreme Court to block abortion pill mail ban
top.description: The emergency filings seek to pause a Fifth Circuit ruling that took effect Friday, requiring mifepristone to be dispensed only in person at clinics nationwide.

top.title: Trump calls US Navy 'like pirates' enforcing Iran blockade
top.description: CENTCOM says 45 ships have been turned back since the blockade began April 13, with Iran losing an estimated $5 billion in oil revenue.

top.title: Spirit Airlines ceases all flights after $500M bailout collapses
top.description: The 34-year-old ultra-low-cost carrier permanently shut down at 3 a.m. Saturday, leaving roughly 17,000 employees without jobs.

top.title: NYPD arrests protesters who blockaded NYSE on May Day
top.description: More than 100 demonstrators tried to shut down every entrance to the exchange as part of nationwide "Workers Over Billionaires" actions.

top.title: Pew poll: Trump approval hits second-term low of 34%
top.description: A new Pew Research Center survey finds sharp declines among Hispanic and young voters who helped power Trump's 2024 win.

top.title: US warns EU allies of arms delivery delays as Iran war drains stockpiles
top.description: The Financial Times reported Friday that warnings now extend beyond Baltic states to include the UK and Poland, as the conflict strains U.S. munitions reserves.

top.title: Bard College president resigns after review of Epstein ties
top.description: Leon Botstein, who led the college for 51 years, stepped down after investigators found he minimized his relationship with convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

top.title: Trump endorses Rep. Barr in Kentucky Senate primary
top.description: The endorsement reshapes the race to replace retiring Sen. Mitch McConnell less than three weeks before the May 19 GOP primary.

top.title: US approves $8.6 billion in arms sales to Middle East allies
top.description: The State Department on Friday cleared weapons deals with Israel, Qatar, Kuwait, and the UAE as the Iran ceasefire remains fragile.

top.title: HHS visa delays leave hundreds of foreign doctors facing deportation
top.description: Physicians contracted to work in underserved U.S. communities may have to leave the country if waiver applications aren't processed before a July 30 deadline.

top.title: Trump says Medicare patients to get GLP-1 drugs by July
top.description: The Bridge program will offer Wegovy and Zepbound to eligible seniors for a $50 monthly copay, operating outside the standard Part D benefit.

top.title: Iran demands six Arab states pay reparations over war
top.description: Tehran's UN envoy rejected Gulf states' self-defense claims, calling their role in the U.S.-Israeli campaign acts of aggression under international law.

top.title: Ex-Rep. Rivera convicted in secret Venezuela lobbying case
top.description: A Miami federal jury found Rivera and associate Esther Nuhfer guilty on all counts, including failing to register as foreign agents for the Maduro government.

top.title: Pentagon to withdraw 5,000 troops from Germany
top.description: The drawdown, set to take place over six to 12 months, escalates Trump's standoff with Chancellor Merz over the war in Iran.

top.title: Kemp rules out redrawing Georgia maps before midterms
top.description: The governor said voting is already underway and he won't cancel the May 19 primary, breaking from GOP counterparts in other states.

top.title: Exxon and Chevron refuse to boost output despite White House pressure
top.description: Both oil supermajors reported sharp profit declines but beat adjusted estimates, doubling down on cash flow over production growth amid the Iran war.

top.title: Trump signs order expanding Cuba sanctions
top.description: The executive order targets individuals and entities supporting Cuba's security apparatus amid an escalating maximum pressure campaign.

top.title: Iran accelerates weapons recovery as ceasefire talks stall, NBC News reports
top.description: U.S. officials say Tehran is exploiting the truce to dig out hidden missiles and drones as the prospect of renewed conflict grows.

top.title: Pentagon strikes AI deals with Nvidia, Microsoft, and four other tech firms for classified work
top.description: The Defense Department now has six major AI partnerships for classified military networks, following its bitter split with Anthropic earlier this year.

top.title: US to shut Gaza ceasefire center as Trump plan stalls
top.description: The CMCC in southern Israel will be folded into a smaller international force, cutting the U.S. military presence from around 190 troops to 40.

top.title: Louisiana suspends House primaries after Supreme Court strikes down map
top.description: Gov. Landry issued an executive order halting the state's congressional races after the court ruled its district map unconstitutional.

top.title: Israel rushed Iron Beam laser system to UAE to counter Iran attacks
top.description: The previously undisclosed deployment marks the first combat export of Israel's laser defense technology, which costs as little as $2 per shot.

top.title: Trump says he's 'not satisfied' with Iran's latest proposal
top.description: The president warned he could "blast the hell out of" Iran if diplomacy fails, as Tehran submitted a new offer through Pakistani mediators.

top.title: Senate rejects House FISA bill, passes 45-day extension
top.description: Lawmakers stripped a central bank digital currency ban that House Republicans attached, calling it a "poison pill" blocking surveillance renewal.

top.title: UAE bans citizens from traveling to Iran, Lebanon, and Iraq
top.description: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs urged Emiratis already in those countries to return immediately, citing regional security concerns amid the ongoing US-Iran conflict.

top.title: Trump to raise tariffs on EU cars and trucks to 25%
top.description: The president accused the EU of not complying with the Turnberry trade deal, which set a 15% ceiling on most goods including automobiles.

top.title: China launches combat patrols near Scarborough Shoal amid US-Philippines drills
top.description: Beijing's military and coast guard conducted naval and air operations in the disputed South China Sea as the largest-ever Balikatan exercises continue nearby.

top.title: Senate unanimously bans members, staff from prediction markets
top.description: The rule change takes effect immediately amid insider trading concerns on platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket.

top.title: Tennessee rushes to redraw maps after SCOTUS voting rights ruling
top.description: The president said Gov. Bill Lee agreed to work on eliminating the state's last Democratic House seat after the court weakened the Voting Rights Act.

top.title: NYT seeks to unseal Epstein's alleged suicide note
top.description: The New York Times filed a court request Thursday to release a note allegedly written by Epstein that has been sealed for nearly seven years.

top.title: Camp Mystic withdraws license bid to reopen after fatal flood
top.description: The all-girls camp in Hunt, Texas, abandoned plans for summer 2026 after weeks of pressure from lawmakers, the lieutenant governor and victims' families.

top.title: Trump says he 'doesn't care' if Powell stays on Fed board
top.description: A day after blasting the Fed chair on social media, the president struck a notably milder tone about Powell's decision to remain as a governor.

top.title: U.S. economy grew 2% in Q1 as jobless claims hit 57-year low
top.description: Thursday's data painted a mixed picture: GDP rebounded but missed forecasts, inflation accelerated sharply, and unemployment filings plunged to levels not seen since 1969.

top.title: Maine Gov. Mills drops Senate bid, clearing path for Platner
top.description: The two-term governor cited insufficient resources to continue against the insurgent oyster farmer, who led by double digits in polling.

top.title: Trump says US has 'already won' Iran war, vows to hold blockade until nuclear deal
top.description: Oil hit wartime highs above $120 a barrel as the president rejected Iran's latest peace proposal and insisted on permanent nuclear guarantees.

top.title: US jobless claims drop to 189,000, near lowest since 1969
top.description: The sharp weekly decline defies expectations of rising layoffs amid tariffs and geopolitical tensions, with filings falling well below forecasts.

top.title: CENTCOM seeks first-ever Dark Eagle hypersonic missile use against Iran
top.description: The request would mark the debut combat deployment of the $41 million-per-missile weapon, which still lacks full operational testing approval.

top.title: DOJ pursuing third indictment of Comey over classified leaks
top.description: Federal prosecutors have revived a probe into the former FBI director's dissemination of documents, days after he was charged over a seashell social media post.

top.title: King Charles honors 9/11 victims in first royal NYC visit in 16 years
top.description: The King and Queen laid a wreath at the National September 11 Memorial on Wednesday, meeting families and first responders ahead of the attacks' 25th anniversary.

top.title: Pentagon releases $400M in Ukraine aid after McConnell pressure campaign
top.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth confirmed the funds were released Wednesday, days after McConnell accused the department of letting the money "collect dust."

top.title: FEMA reinstates whistleblowers sidelined under Noem
top.description: Fifteen employees who signed the "Katrina Declaration" return to work as the agency prepares for hurricane season and the FIFA World Cup.

top.title: London stabbing of 2 Jewish men declared terrorist attack
top.description: Police arrested a 45-year-old suspect after two men were knifed in Golders Green, prompting PM Starmer to convene a COBRA meeting.

top.title: USS Gerald R. Ford to leave Middle East after 10-month deployment
top.description: The carrier's departure will reduce U.S. naval firepower in the region from three carrier strike groups to two amid the ongoing Iran conflict.

top.title: Trump says WHCA dinner agent was shot by suspect, not friendly fire
top.description: Ballistic analysis traced the round that struck the Secret Service agent's vest to Cole Allen's shotgun, ending days of speculation.

top.title: Fed holds rates steady with most dissent since 1992
top.description: The 8-4 vote split came at what is likely Chair Powell's final meeting, as oil-driven inflation clouds the rate outlook.

top.title: Bondi agrees to May 29 deposition in House Epstein probe
top.description: Democrats introduced a contempt resolution hours before the committee confirmed the former attorney general's new testimony date.

top.title: Trump taps Nicole Saphier as surgeon general after pulling Casey Means
top.description: Saphier, a radiologist at Memorial Sloan Kettering and former Fox News contributor, is Trump's third pick for the role in his second term.

top.title: Hegseth tells Congress Iran's nuclear sites were 'obliterated'
top.description: The defense secretary faced sharp pushback at a House hearing over why the U.S. remains at war if Iran's nuclear facilities were already destroyed.

top.title: France unveils roadmap to end all fossil fuel use by 2050
top.description: The plan, presented at a global summit in Colombia, sets deadlines to phase out coal by 2030, oil by 2045 and gas by 2050.

top.title: Iran's Khamenei vows to defend nuclear capabilities after Trump-Putin call
top.description: Iran's supreme leader called nuclear and missile programs "national assets," rejecting U.S. pressure as Trump pushes for a deal on Tehran's enriched uranium.

top.title: Hegseth calls war critics 'biggest adversary' in fiery congressional testimony
top.description: The defense secretary faced lawmakers for the first time since the Iran war began, as Pentagon revealed the conflict has cost $25 billion.

top.title: Adelaide researchers map path to turning plastic into hydrogen fuel
top.description: A new roadmap details how sunlight-powered photoreforming could convert waste plastics into clean hydrogen and industrial chemicals.

top.title: Israel sets two-week deadline for Lebanon deal, warns of escalation
top.description: Trump urged Netanyahu to conduct "surgical" strikes as Israel threatens to resume intensive operations if no permanent agreement is reached by mid-May.

top.title: EU finds Meta failed to block under-13s from Instagram, Facebook
top.description: The European Commission issued preliminary findings that Meta violated the Digital Services Act by not preventing children under 13 from accessing its platforms.

top.title: Artemis II capsule returns to Kennedy as Artemis III rocket assembly begins
top.description: NASA's Orion crew module arrived back at its launch site Tuesday while the next moon rocket's core stage entered the Vehicle Assembly Building.

top.title: Appeals court rejects Trump bid to revisit E. Jean Carroll verdict
top.description: The divided 2nd Circuit ruling leaves the Supreme Court as Trump's last option to challenge two jury verdicts totaling more than $88 million.

top.title: King Charles urges unity in rare address to Congress
top.description: The British monarch became only the second to speak before U.S. lawmakers, calling the alliance "one of the greatest partnerships in human history."

top.title: Pentagon asks Congress to formally rename DOD the Department of War
top.description: The legislative proposal would require 7,600 changes to existing law and has already cost an estimated $52 million, according to the Pentagon.

top.title: DOJ unveils sweeping rollback of gun regulations
top.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche called the 34 proposed and final rule changes the most comprehensive reform package in ATF history.

top.title: Soldier pleads not guilty to betting on Maduro raid with classified intel
top.description: Master Sgt. Gannon Ken Van Dyke faces five federal charges in what prosecutors call the first insider trading case involving a prediction market.

top.title: GAO agrees to review DOJ's handling of Epstein files
top.description: The independent watchdog's probe joins a DOJ inspector general audit and a new federal lawsuit over alleged noncompliance with the transparency law.

top.title: Trump calls UAE's exit from OPEC 'great' for fuel prices
top.description: The president praised the move as the White House met with oil executives to discuss sustaining the Iran blockade while shielding consumers.

top.title: Over 23,000 Iranian businesses destroyed as war's economic toll mounts
top.description: Two million jobs have already been lost, with half of Iran's workforce at risk as airstrikes devastate industry and inflation hits 72%.

top.title: FBI raids 22 Minneapolis childcare centers in fraud probe
top.description: No arrests were made, but agents executed search warrants at facilities tied to alleged COVID-era social services fraud totaling billions.

top.title: Trump and Putin discuss Iran, Ukraine in 90-minute call
top.description: The 90-minute conversation also covered the Iran conflict and the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting, the Kremlin said.

top.title: SpaceX ties Musk's pay to Mars colony, $7.5T valuation
top.description: A Reuters analysis reveals the compensation plan, disclosed in SpaceX's draft IPO prospectus, would award Musk 200 million super-voting shares.

top.title: SPLC files motions challenging DOJ fraud case, seeks grand jury transcripts
top.description: The civil rights group says its informant program was known to law enforcement and accuses acting AG Blanche of making false public statements.

top.title: Warsh clears Senate committee, heads to full vote
top.description: The Senate Banking Committee voted 13-11 along party lines to advance Trump's Fed chair nominee, putting him on track to succeed Powell before May 15.

top.title: Google signs classified AI deal with Pentagon despite employee protests
top.description: The agreement, reported Tuesday by The Information, came one day after more than 600 workers urged CEO Sundar Pichai to reject the contract.

top.title: Iran war energy shock pushes millions back to charcoal and firewood
top.description: Soaring fuel costs from the Strait of Hormuz closure are forcing families across Africa and South Asia to abandon cleaner cooking fuels, accelerating deforestation.

top.title: Trump meets with oil execs as Iran blockade stalemate drags on
top.description: The White House session with Chevron's CEO and other energy leaders came as Trump directed aides to prepare for a prolonged Strait of Hormuz naval blockade.

top.title: Iraq taps political newcomer al-Zaidi as prime minister-designate
top.description: President Amedi designated 40-year-old businessman Ali al-Zaidi to form a government after U.S. pressure blocked a pro-Iran candidate.

top.title: OpenAI misses revenue and user targets ahead of IPO
top.description: The shortfalls intensify doubts about the AI company's ability to sustain massive infrastructure spending as it prepares for a planned Q4 listing.

top.title: Iran tensions shadow White House state dinner for British royals
top.description: During Tuesday's state dinner, Trump said King Charles "agrees" Iran must never have a nuclear weapon, prompting a Palace response over the king's political neutrality.

top.title: Airbnb.org offers free housing to Oklahomans displaced by tornadoes
top.description: The initiative, announced Saturday in partnership with Heartline 211, follows a week of severe storms that damaged dozens of homes across the state.

top.title: Appeals court lets Pentagon require escorts for journalists
top.description: A divided D.C. Circuit panel stayed a lower court order, allowing the Defense Department to mandate journalist escorts while it appeals.

top.title: State Dept. to issue passports with Trump's portrait for America's 250th birthday
top.description: The limited-edition design makes Trump the first sitting president featured in a U.S. passport, available by request in Washington.

top.title: First LNG tanker crosses Hormuz since Iran war began
top.description: Ship-tracking data shows the vessel near India after weeks without a signal, but analysts caution one crossing doesn't signal a broader reopening.

top.title: Judge pauses order requiring UPenn to share Jewish staff data with EEOC
top.description: Penn will not have to turn over names of employees in Jewish organizations while it appeals to the Third Circuit.

top.title: Supreme Court to hear challenge to Trump's TPS termination for Haitians, Syrians
top.description: Oral arguments Wednesday in two consolidated cases could shape the future of temporary legal protections for more than 1 million immigrants from 17 countries.

top.title: OpenAI, Microsoft end exclusive IP deal in revised pact
top.description: The amended agreement lets OpenAI serve customers on any cloud platform and caps the revenue share it must pay Microsoft through 2030.

top.title: Iran's Araghchi meets Putin as US peace talks stall
top.description: Iran's foreign minister blamed Washington's "excessive demands" for the collapse of negotiations after arriving in St. Petersburg for talks with Putin.

top.title: Trump signs law lifting mining ban near Boundary Waters
top.description: The legislation reverses Biden-era protections on 225,000 acres of Minnesota's Superior National Forest, benefiting Chilean-owned Twin Metals.

top.title: China blocks Meta's $2B acquisition of AI startup Manus
top.description: The NDRC ordered the deal's cancellation Monday, citing laws on technology exports, as Beijing cracks down on "Singapore washing" strategies.

top.title: China quietly expands economic pressure toolkit during trade truce
top.description: Beijing has enacted new laws, tightened export controls, and banned foreign tech from key sectors even as it prepares for a mid-May summit with Washington.

top.title: WHCD shooter charged with attempting to assassinate Trump
top.description: Cole Tomas Allen was arraigned Monday on three federal counts, including attempted assassination, after Saturday's attack at the Washington Hilton.

top.title: Hardliner set to replace Ghalibaf in Iran nuclear talks
top.description: A leaked secret letter to Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei exposed a rift over whether Tehran should negotiate its nuclear program with the U.S.

top.title: NYT traces U.S. Mint gold to Colombian drug cartel
top.description: An investigation found the Mint buys gold from cartel-controlled mines and foreign pawn shops, violating a 1985 law requiring domestic sourcing.

top.title: Reuters uncovers Trump push for federal election control in 8 states
top.description: The investigation found the administration using FBI raids, DOJ lawsuits, and demands for voter data to assert control ahead of November's midterms.

top.title: Global military spending hit record $2.89 trillion in 2025, SIPRI reports
top.description: Europe's 14% surge and Asia's fastest growth since 2009 drove the 11th straight annual increase, even as U.S. spending fell.

top.title: Zelenskyy says EU accession talks could open in June
top.description: Ukraine and Moldova pressed the EU to begin formal membership negotiations, with Zelenskyy citing late May or June as the expected timeline.

top.title: Trump calls CBS anchor 'a disgrace' over WHCD shooter manifesto question
top.description: The president erupted at Norah O'Donnell during a Sunday "60 Minutes" interview after she read passages from the gunman's anti-Trump writings.

top.title: Iran refuses talks unless US lifts naval blockade
top.description: Tehran demands removal of the port blockade before resuming negotiations, as the U.S. Navy intercepts another sanctioned vessel.

top.title: Zelenskyy says EU loan triggered Russia's 20-hour assault on Dnipro
top.description: Speaking alongside Moldova's president in Kyiv, Zelenskyy called the deadly barrage a direct reaction to the EU unblocking €90 billion in support.

top.title: California billionaire tax secures enough signatures for November ballot
top.description: Backers say more than 1.5 million people signed the petition, far exceeding the roughly 875,000 valid signatures required.

top.title: Trump tells Iran to call if it wants peace talks
top.description: The president's remarks on Sunday came a day after he scrapped a planned trip by envoys Witkoff and Kushner to Pakistan for negotiations.

top.title: Thousands gather on National Mall to honor Virginia Giuffre
top.description: The "Butterfly Vigil" marks one year since the prominent Epstein accuser died by suicide at age 41 in Australia.

top.title: Macron calls EU defense clause 'stronger' than NATO's
top.description: The French president used an Athens visit to push European self-reliance while insisting the EU's defense buildup is not meant to replace NATO.

top.title: Suspect in custody as Trump plans news conference after incident
top.description: A suspect was apprehended and no injuries were reported after gunfire erupted at the Washington Hilton during the annual event Saturday night.

top.title: Iran threatens 'decisive' military response to US Hormuz blockade
top.description: Tehran's top military command said forces are at their "highest level of readiness" as three US carrier strike groups converge on the region.

top.title: Chernobyl anniversary marked as IAEA warns damaged shield needs repair
top.description: Ukraine commemorates the 1986 disaster on Sunday with the facility's protective shield still damaged from a 2025 Russian drone strike.

top.title: White House official says Patel firing 'only a matter of time'
top.description: A top White House official told Politico that FBI Director Kash Patel is likely the next Cabinet member to be removed amid mounting scandals.

top.title: UK and Finland condemn Israeli killing of journalist in Lebanon as 'intolerable'
top.description: The joint statement from the Media Freedom Coalition co-chairs follows the death of Al-Akhbar reporter Amal Khalil in an April 22 airstrike.

top.title: Space Force awards $3.2B to 12 firms for Golden Dome interceptors
top.description: The contracts task defense giants and startups with building space-based missile interceptors, a technically unproven pillar of the $185 billion shield.

top.title: Trump fires all members of National Science Board
top.description: The 25-member body governs the NSF and advises Congress on science policy. It was created by a 1950 federal law.

top.title: US orders global diplomatic push over alleged Chinese AI theft
top.description: The State Department cable names DeepSeek, Moonshot AI and MiniMax, and comes hours after DeepSeek released its V4 model.

top.title: Palestinians vote in first Gaza election in 20 years
top.description: Polls opened Saturday in Deir al-Balah and the West Bank for municipal elections, with Hamas effectively barred under a decree by President Abbas.

top.title: Trump's ballroom builder got secret $17.4M no-bid deal
top.description: Clark Construction, already tapped for Trump's White House ballroom, received a no-bid contract for Lafayette Park fountains at five times the prior cost estimate.

top.title: Warren, Durbin warn DOJ could relaunch Powell probe at will
top.description: Democratic senators say Pirro's decision to close the Fed investigation is only a "temporary pause," leaving the door open for a politically motivated revival.

top.title: Hegseth brushes off Pope Leo's criticism of Iran war
top.description: The defense secretary told reporters the military follows "the orders of the president," as the U.S.-Vatican rift over the conflict deepens.

top.title: Judge questions jurisdiction over Trump's $10B IRS suit
top.description: Judge Kathleen Williams ordered briefings on whether the case can proceed when the president controls both sides of the litigation.

top.title: Nvidia surges toward record high as chip rally lifts sector
top.description: Intel's earnings beat sparked a broad semiconductor rally, pushing Nvidia shares near their all-time closing high set in October 2025.

top.title: Atlantic reporter says new sources emerged after Patel's $250M lawsuit
top.description: FBI Director Kash Patel's defamation suit appears to have backfired as journalist Sarah Fitzpatrick says she's been "inundated" with corroborating sources.

top.title: Trump administration moves to again revoke parole for CBP One migrants
top.description: Court filings show new termination notices are being issued weeks after a judge ruled the first mass revocation was unlawful.

top.title: Iran claims seized cargo ship was carrying dialysis supplies, demands release
top.description: Tehran's UN mission and the Iranian Red Crescent say the Touska was carrying critical kidney dialysis materials when the US Navy seized it April 19.

top.title: Netanyahu reveals secret prostate cancer diagnosis and treatment
top.description: The Israeli prime minister said Friday he quietly underwent radiation therapy months ago and is now cancer-free and in "excellent physical condition."

top.title: New York sues to reclaim $73M in highway funds cut by Trump administration
top.description: Attorney General Letitia James and Gov. Hochul filed suit Friday after the DOT withheld funding over a dispute about commercial driver's licenses.

top.title: Macron rejects US approach to Iran at EU summit
top.description: The French president called for diplomacy over military pressure, warning that the Middle East conflict is destabilizing the global economy.

top.title: California battery storage boom faces growing revolt from fire-wary communities
top.description: Residents are blocking projects, filing lawsuits, and pushing moratoriums as developers seek sites near neighborhoods and wildfire zones.

top.title: D.C. Circuit strikes down Trump's asylum ban at border
top.description: The 2-1 ruling sets up a likely Supreme Court clash over whether the president can unilaterally bar migrants from seeking asylum.

top.title: House panel advances bill cutting US UN funding by 78%
top.description: The FY2027 spending bill would slash contributions to international organizations and zero out US dues for the UN regular budget.

top.title: FDA clears first gene therapy for inherited hearing loss
top.description: Regeneron says it will provide Otarmeni, which restored hearing in children born deaf, for free to eligible U.S. patients.

top.title: DOJ expands federal execution methods to include firing squads
top.description: Acting AG Todd Blanche directed the Bureau of Prisons to add firing squads and reinstate the lethal injection protocol from Trump's first term.

top.title: Iran war strains US ability to arm and defend Taiwan
top.description: A naval blockade, depleted munitions, and a stalled $14 billion arms deal are stretching Pentagon resources as China watches closely.

top.title: DOJ targets 384 citizens for denaturalization in 'first wave'
top.description: Prosecutors in 39 regional offices will pursue citizenship revocation cases, a sharp escalation from the historical average of 11 cases per year.

top.title: 77% of voters blame Trump for gas price surge, poll finds
top.description: A Reuters/Ipsos poll released Friday shows broad voter frustration over fuel costs that have risen more than 30% since the Iran war began.

top.title: Meta to cut 8,000 jobs starting May 20, more layoffs planned for 2026
top.description: The first wave targets about 10% of Meta's global workforce as the company pivots toward AI and away from divisions like Reality Labs.

top.title: Wall Street slips from record highs as oil tops $103
top.description: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq pulled back Thursday as Strait of Hormuz disruptions pushed Brent crude higher and U.S.-Iran peace talks stalled.

top.title: Offshore wind farms expand off Rhode Island despite Trump administration's efforts to block them
top.description: Three wind projects now operate in waters off Rhode Island as courts continue to reject federal attempts to halt the industry.

top.title: JetBlue sued over alleged use of personal data to set fares
top.description: A class action filed in Brooklyn federal court claims the airline uses tracking technology and customer data to inflate ticket prices.

top.title: Microsoft offers first voluntary buyout in its 51-year history
top.description: About 7% of U.S. employees are eligible for the retirement packages as the company continues reshaping its workforce around AI.

top.title: Trump threatens UK with tariffs over digital services tax
top.description: The warning came as new data showed the UK collected £944 million from the levy in 2025-26, a 17% increase from the prior year.

top.title: France probes weather sensor tampering tied to Polymarket bets
top.description: Météo-France filed a criminal complaint after suspicious temperature spikes at a Paris airport sensor aligned with $34,000 in prediction market wins.

top.title: California's wildfire deforestation rivals global hotspots
top.description: California lost conifer forest at up to three times the global average rate from 2001 to 2023, with reforestation efforts barely keeping pace, according to researchers.

top.title: Trump weighs doubling refugee cap for white South Africans
top.description: The administration is discussing adding 10,000 spots to the current 7,500 ceiling, nearly all earmarked for Afrikaners, according to Reuters.

top.title: EU formally approves €90B Ukraine loan, new Russia sanctions
top.description: Leaders gathering in Cyprus for a two-day summit cleared the long-stalled aid package and a 20th round of sanctions targeting Moscow's energy and financial sectors.

top.title: Oklo and NVIDIA team up with Los Alamos on nuclear fuel for AI factories
top.description: The partnership combines Oklo's reactor technology, NVIDIA's AI infrastructure, and the lab's materials science expertise to support the DOE's Genesis Mission.

top.title: DOJ lost 11,200 employees despite tough-on-crime vows
top.description: Internal records obtained by Reuters show the Justice Department's workforce shrank across nearly every division, including the FBI, DEA and ATF.

top.title: Interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS traced to cold, ancient corner of the Milky Way
top.description: Scientists say the third confirmed interstellar visitor likely formed up to 11 billion years ago in a frigid, isolated region before its host star's planetary system took shape.

top.title: Senate adopts GOP budget plan to fund ICE, Border Patrol
top.description: The 50-48 vote early Thursday advances a partisan $70 billion plan to reopen DHS, bypassing Democrats through budget reconciliation.

top.title: China warns of 'critical juncture' as Iran ceasefire talks falter
top.description: Beijing urged all parties to prevent a return to war after scheduled U.S.-Iran peace talks in Pakistan stalled and Trump extended the ceasefire while maintaining a naval blockade.

top.title: SpaceX reveals in-house GPU plans in pre-IPO filing
top.description: The company flagged chip supply risks and "substantial capital expenditures" for AI hardware as it prepares for a potential record-setting public offering.

top.title: Tesla raises capex plan to $25 billion for AI push
top.description: The EV maker now expects negative free cash flow for the rest of 2026 as it triples spending on robots, autonomy and AI infrastructure.

top.title: DOJ reclassifies medical marijuana to Schedule III
top.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche signed the order Thursday, delivering on Trump's December executive order and marking the biggest federal marijuana policy shift since 1970.

top.title: Nuclear leaders convene in Busan, framing reactors as AI-era infrastructure
top.description: Some 19,000 participants from 19 countries gathered in South Korea this week to discuss nuclear power's role in meeting surging AI electricity demand.

top.title: IDF removes 40 Israeli settlers who crossed into Syria
top.description: The far-right Pioneers of Bashan group barricaded themselves on a rooftop in a Syrian village, demanding approval for Jewish settlements.

top.title: Trump says China helped push Iran toward ceasefire talks
top.description: Beijing used its leverage as Iran's top oil buyer to urge Tehran back to the negotiating table, diplomats told the Associated Press.
